content.text:
```json
{
  "module": "scheduling",
  "scores": {
    "visual_polish": 28,
    "domain_depth": 32,
    "data_richness": 22,
    "interaction_quality": 20,
    "ai_integration": 8,
    "mobile_first": 15,
    "performance": 30,
    "typescript_quality": 35,
    "error_handling": 22,
    "realtime": 25,
    "accessibility": 12,
    "security": 30,
    "differentiation": 15,
    "enterprise_readiness": 18
  },
  "overall_score": 22,
  "issues": [
    {
      "id": "scheduling-C3-001",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "error_handling",
      "title": "Schedule page has no loading or empty state",
      "file": "src/pages/Schedule.tsx",
      "description": "The Schedule page renders no skeleton loader during data fetch and shows a blank area when no schedule phases exist. Per PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA item 11, the empty state must say 'Build your schedule to track every phase' with CTAs for 'Create First Phase' and 'Import from P6/MS Project'. Loading must show skeleton Gantt placeholder bars.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Schedule.tsx, in the default exported Schedule component: BEFORE: The component renders content directly without checking loading or empty states, showing a blank page when data is loading or when no schedule phases exist. AFTER: 1) When isLoading is true, render a skeleton loader consisting of 4 horizontal bars (heights 32px, widths 60%/45%/75%/30%) with pulsing animation matching the existing skeleton pattern in other pages, wrapped in a Card with style={{ padding: theme.spacing.lg }}. 2) When data is loaded but the activities array is empty, render an empty state inside a Card with: a calendar icon (from lucide-react Calendar), heading text 'Build your schedule to track every phase from mobilization to closeout', and two buttons: a primary Btn 'Create First Phase' and a secondary outlined Btn 'Import from P6 / MS Project'. Use theme.colors and theme.spacing tokens for all styles. 3) When there is an error, show an inline error banner with the error message and a 'Retry' button that calls refetch. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "scheduling-C3-002",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "accessibility",
      "title": "Gantt chart and schedule table lack ARIA attributes",
      "file": "src/pages/Schedule.tsx",
      "description": "The schedule page has no aria-labels on interactive elements, no role attributes on the Gantt chart container, and no aria-live region for status updates. Screen readers cannot navigate the schedule. Keyboard navigation between phases is absent.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Schedule.tsx, in the default exported Schedule component: BEFORE: The schedule table/Gantt container has no ARIA attributes. Table rows have no aria-label. Status changes are not announced. There are no role attributes on the chart container. AFTER: 1) Add role='table' and aria-label='Project schedule' to the main schedule container div. 2) Add role='row' to each phase/activity row element. 3) Add aria-label to each row using the pattern `${activity.name}, ${activity.percent_complete}% complete, ${activity.status}`. 4) Add role='columnheader' and scope='col' to all table header cells. 5) Add an aria-live='polite' div (visually hidden with position absolute, width 1px, height 1px, overflow hidden) that announces when activities are updated, e.g., 'Schedule updated: {name} is now {status}'. 6) Ensure all clickable elements (phase rows, toolbar buttons) have tabIndex={0} and onKeyDown handlers that trigger onClick on Enter/Space. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "scheduling-C3-003",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "mobile_first",
      "title": "Schedule page is unusable on mobile viewports",
      "file": "src/pages/Schedule.tsx",
      "description": "Per PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA item 12, on mobile the Gantt should be replaced with a timeline list view where each phase is a card with start/end dates, progress bar, and status. Currently the Gantt chart overflows horizontally with no responsive fallback, making it unusable on 375px screens.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Schedule.tsx, in the default exported Schedule component: BEFORE: The Gantt chart renders at a fixed width that overflows on mobile. There is no responsive breakpoint or alternative layout for screens under 768px. AFTER: 1) Add a state variable `const isMobile = useMediaQuery('(max-width: 768px)')` using a simple useMediaQuery hook (add it inline at the top of the file: `function useMediaQuery(query: string) { const [matches, setMatches] = useState(false); useEffect(() => { const mql = window.matchMedia(query); setMatches(mql.matches); const handler = (e: MediaQueryListEvent) => setMatches(e.matches); mql.addEventListener('change', handler); return () => mql.removeEventListener('change', handler); }, [query]); return matches; }`). 2) When isMobile is true, instead of rendering the Gantt chart, render a vertical list of cards. Each card shows: activity name (16px, fontWeight 600), a row with start date and end date formatted as 'MMM DD', a progress bar (height 6px, borderRadius 3px, background theme.colors.border, fill using theme.colors.primary at percent_complete width), status Tag component, and percent_complete text. Cards should have padding 16px, marginBottom 12px, background white, borderRadius 12px, border 1px solid theme.colors.border. 3) Metric cards at top should stack to a single column grid on mobile: gridTemplateColumns '1fr'. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "scheduling-C3-004",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "domain_depth",
      "title": "No baseline vs actual comparison on Gantt",
      "file": "src/pages/Schedule.tsx",
      "description": "Per PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA item 8 and MODULE_SPECIFICATIONS, the schedule must show baseline dates as ghost bars behind actual bars. The mapScheduleActivityRow in schedule.ts maps baseline_start and baseline_end but the UI never renders them. This is a core construction PM requirement for tracking slippage.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Schedule.tsx, in the Gantt bar rendering section (where activity bars are drawn): BEFORE: Each activity renders a single colored bar based on start_date and finish_date. The baseline_start and baseline_finish fields from the activity data are ignored and never displayed. AFTER: 1) For each activity that has both baseline_start and baseline_finish values (not null), render a ghost bar BEHIND the actual bar. The ghost bar should use background: theme.colors.border (light gray), opacity: 0.5, height: 8px (thinner than the actual bar), borderRadius: 4px, positioned at the same vertical center as the actual bar. 2) The actual bar renders on top with its current styling at full height. 3) Add a legend item in the toolbar area: a small gray rectangle labeled 'Baseline' and a colored rectangle labeled 'Actual'. 4) When baseline and actual diverge, calculate the variance in days as `Math.ceil((new Date(activity.finish_date).getTime() - new Date(activity.baseline_finish).getTime()) / 86400000)` and show it as a small badge on the bar: red for positive (late), green for negative (ahead), e.g., '+3d' or '-1d'. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "scheduling-C3-005",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "typescript_quality",
      "title": "mapScheduleActivityRow has incomplete type safety and hardcoded null fields",
      "file": "src/api/endpoints/schedule.ts",
      "description": "The mapScheduleActivityRow function returns hardcoded null for actual_start, actual_finish, wbs_code, trade, assigned_sub_id, and hardcoded false for is_milestone and outdoor_activity. The successor_ids array is always empty even though it could be computed from the dependency graph. Also planned_percent_complete is always 0. These stale defaults mask missing data and prevent the UI from rendering meaningful information.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/api/endpoints/schedule.ts, in the mapScheduleActivityRow function: BEFORE: The function hardcodes actual_start: null, actual_finish: null, planned_percent_complete: 0, successor_ids: [], is_milestone: false, wbs_code: null, trade: null, assigned_sub_id: null, outdoor_activity: false regardless of what the database row contains. AFTER: 1) Update the SCHEDULE_SELECT constant to also include 'actual_start, actual_finish, wbs_code, trade, assigned_sub_id, is_milestone, outdoor_activity, planned_percent_complete' (add these columns). 2) Update the RawRow type to include these optional fields: actual_start?: string | null, actual_finish?: string | null, wbs_code?: string | null, trade?: string | null, assigned_sub_id?: string | null, is_milestone?: boolean | null, outdoor_activity?: boolean | null, planned_percent_complete?: number | null. 3) In mapScheduleActivityRow, read from the row instead of hardcoding: actual_start: row.actual_start ?? null, actual_finish: row.actual_finish ?? null, planned_percent_complete: row.planned_percent_complete ?? 0, is_milestone: row.is_milestone ?? false, wbs_code: row.wbs_code ?? null, trade: row.trade ?? null, assigned_sub_id: row.assigned_sub_id ?? null, outdoor_activity: row.outdoor_activity ?? false. Keep successor_ids: [] as is since it requires cross-referencing. 4) If these columns do not exist in the schedule_phases table, wrap the select in a try-catch and fall back to the current hardcoded defaults so the build does not break. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    }
  ],
  "invented_features": [],
  "competitive_intel": "Procore's schedule module integrates directly with P6 and MS Project via file import and displays baseline vs actual with earned value metrics. Fieldwire has no schedule capability at all. Autodesk Build has schedule but no AI delay prediction. SiteSync's schedule page currently lacks basic construction PM features (baseline comparison, critical path highlighting, import) that are table stakes for any GC managing a $50M+ project. The empty Gantt with no data is the weakest page in the application and would immediately disqualify SiteSync in any demo against Procore.",
  "summary": "The Schedule module is the weakest area of SiteSync at 22/100. It fails on fundamental construction PM requirements: no loading or empty states (blank page on first visit), no baseline vs actual comparison (the most requested schedule feature by PMs), completely unusable on mobile (Gantt overflows with no responsive fallback), zero accessibility support (screen readers cannot navigate the schedule), and incomplete type safety in the API layer where critical fields are hardcoded to null instead of reading from the database. The five surgical fixes target: empty/loading states per acceptance criteria, ARIA attributes for accessibility compliance, mobile responsive fallback from Gantt to card list, baseline ghost bars for schedule variance visualization, and type safety cleanup in the data mapping layer. These fixes address the most impactful gaps without adding new features."
}
```
